MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christopher Lamerdin for The Attorney General's Office. Robert Cowan for Constance Carter.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to Mr. Cowan's litigation fees. Both matters were taken under submission. The Court will issue an order for funds to be released/withdrawn from the Court and distributed to the Charity.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Reinstate Trustee Constance Carter For The Limited Purpose Of Distributing The Proceeds Of The Douimbia Trust To Larkin Street Youth Services
Off Calendar Oct-18-2011 Continued To Nov-01-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Susan Kawala appearing for Attorney Christopher Lamerdin for Attorney General's Office. Robert Cowan for Constance Carter.

Mr. Cowan stated that execution of the deed with regard to the Texas property was not accomplished. The Court will prepare an order reinstating Ms. Carter for that purpose. Mr. Cowan also spoke of Ms. Malveux's motion, his fees and informed the Court that he filed a Second Amended Petition for Final Distribution today. Both matters were continued to November 1, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to see if Mr. Lamerdin wants to file anything with regard to said petition (to be filed by October 28, 2011). If nothing is filed by that date, the matter be deemed under submission and the Court will direct Mr. Lamerdin to distribute all checks by way of a Court order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Whitehead.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Reinstate Trustee Constance Carter For The Limited Purpose Of Distributing The Proceeds Of The Douimbia Trust To Larkin Street Youth Services
Off Calendar Aug-23-2011 Continued To Oct-18-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Cowan for Constance Carter. Christopher Lamerdin for Attorney General's Office. Marianne Malveaux in Propria Persona.

Status of accounting of the Wells Fargo Bank account was taken off calendar. The Court will issue an order reinstating Ms. Carter as Trustee solely for the purpose of transferring the Texas property, removing the funds from Chase Bank and depositing them with the Court or an Attorney General's account. The Petition to Reinstate Trustee Constance Carter For The Purpose of Distributing Proceeds to Larkin Street Youth Services and the Accounting Petition Re Final Distribution were continued to October 18, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

Off Calendar Aug-23-2011 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-12-2011 Continued To Aug-23-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Cowan for Constance Carter. Marianne Malveaux in Propria Persona. Christopher Lamerdin for The Attorney General's Office.

The Court stated that it read Judge Karnow's statement of decision which awarded Ms. Malveaux $15,750.00 on the basis of a settlement agreement dated February 24, 2010 entered into with Ms. Carter and approved by Mr. Cowan, and that Judge Karnow asked the Parties to brief him on what happened in the Probate Court on May 24, 2011 since he seemed to be under the impression that there was a petition to approve the settlement. Mr. Cowan explained what his understanding of the settlement agreement was, stated that he was not aware that he had to obtain approval and present the settlement agreement to the Probate Court and that the Probate Court approved the litigation. Ms. Malveaux spoke of Mr. Cowan's deception of the Court into pay fees, stated that litigation was unnecessary, that the expense of litigation to herself and the Trust was unnecessary and that she filed an objection to Mr. Cowan's fees because of breach of the agreement. The matter was continued to August 23, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Cowan is to file a copy of the settlement agreement and to break out fees between litigation with Ms. Malveaux and Trust issues by July 29, 2011.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Filing Of Blocked Account Receipts
Off Calendar May-04-2011 Continued To May-24-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Presentation Of The New Recipient Of The Funds And For Filing Of A Declaration By Mr. Cowan Regarding The Set Aside Amount For Ms. Malveaux'S Claim
Off Calendar May-04-2011 Continued To May-24-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Cowan for Constatnce Carter. Christopher Lamerdin for Attorney General's Office.

The Court stated that it does not have to reinstate Ms. Carter as Trustee for the purpose of distributing funds from the Trust to the recipient Larkin Street Youth Services and that it could use the Judicial Counsel form (Order to Withdraw Funds From Blocked Account) for that purpose. The Court also stated that an adequate reserve was needed to address any remaining fees, that an accounting was not filed with regard to the activity in the Wells Fargo account and questioned whether Ms. Carter should be surcharged in the amount of $13,284.00. Mr. Cowan presented a copy of the receipt for the blocked account for a deposit in the amount of $13,000.00 showing a balance of $223,000.00, stated that Ms. Cowan was caught up in a scam, that she was unable to give an account as to the $13,000.00 she expended, that her finances were in total disarray and that Ms. Carter was willing to pay $300.00 per month but that he was not sure of her ability to do so. Mr. Cowan also stated that he expects his fees to be in the amount of $15,000.00 in addition to his prior fees of $8,000.00, that a motion was set for May 19, 2011 and a trial date of May 23, 2011 in the Malveaux matter and that the Texas property still needed to be distributed. The matter was continued to May 24, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Lamerdin is to prepare the order for withdrawal of funds from the blocked account. The Court stated that a petition for Mr. Cowan's fees and distribution of the Texas property is needed. The Court also stated that it may have to make some distinction between services provided to defend Ms. Carter and the services provided to benefit the Trust.
Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Trustee'S Account For The Wells Fargo Bank Account Funds
Off Calendar Feb-09-2011 Continued To May-04-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Filing Of Blocked Account Receipts
Off Calendar Feb-09-2011 Continued To May-04-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Cowan for Constance Carter. Christopher Lamerdin for Attorney General's Office.

Mr. Cowan stated that Ms. Carter was having health issues, that Wells Fargo has no open bank accounts, that he received a cashiers check in the amount of $13,286.00, that there were conflicting accounts as to what happened to the balance and that Ms. Carter was considering a home loan to pay back the funds. Mr. Lamerdin stated that he wanted more time to conduct a further investigation of the new recipient. The Court directed Mr. Cowan to deposit the $13,286.00 to the Chase blocked account and file a receipt for deposit of said funds. The matter was continued to May 4, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The February 16th hearing date was also continued to that date. Mr. Cowan will prepare an order directing Chase bank to to turn over the bank account number and provide any and all bank activity.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Presentation Of The New Recipient Of The Funds And For Filing Of A Declaration By Mr. Cowan Regarding The Set Aside Amount For Ms. Malveaux'S Claim
Off Calendar Feb-02-2011 Continued To Feb-09-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Cowen for Constance Carter. Terrence Halinan for Constance Carter. Christopher Lamerdin for Attorney General's Office. Janice McEldry (Grandaughter of Ms. Carter) also present.

Mr. Halinan stated that he has not received a signed Substition of Attorney by Ms. Carter. Mr. Cowan stated that he does not have access to any of the bank accounts, that Ms Carter has made herself unavailable to him and that he does not know what happened to the Wells Fargo account. The Court will entertain an ex parte request to block any accounts at Chase bank in the name of the Trust or any accounts in Ms. Carter's name as Trustee of the Trust. Mr. Cowan is to file said ex parte petition. The Court suspended Constance Carter's powers as Trustee of the Trust (Mr. Cowan is to prepare said order). The Court informed Counsel of the February 9th (status of blocked account receipts) and the February 16th (status of Trustee's account for Wells Fargo Bank account) hearing dates. Mr. Cowan stated that he has not completed his investigation of the proposed Beneficiary, that the Texas property still needed to be disposed of and that he was unable to obtain a Judicial Counsel receipt form for the blocked account without a court order. Mr. Lamerdin will take an active roll in assisting with a proposed Beneficiary. Mr. Lamerdin stated that the alternate Trustee was not interested in serving/wanting to act. The matters were continued to February 9, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Presentation Of The New Recipient Of The Funds And For Filing Of A Declaration By Mr. Cowan Regarding The Set Aside Amount For Ms. Malveaux'S Claim
Off Calendar Jan-26-2011 Continued To Feb-02-11 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christopher Lamerdin for Attorney General's Office. Robert Cowan for and with Constatnce Carter. Regnaldo Woods (Interested Party) also present.

Mr. Cowan advised the Court that he received a Substitution of Attorney substituting Ms. Carter in as Propria Persona, and expressed concerns about her inability to defend this matter as well as the civil matter. Mr. Cowan also stated that the $26,000.00 he re-deposited into the Wells Fargo account was withdrawn and that the account was closed. Mr. Cowan also spoke of three withdrawals and provided information on the Chase blocked account. Ms. Carter stated that she used some of the money to pay for expenses owed for repairs on the house, that she had receipts and that there was $19,000.00 left. The Court ordered Ms. Carter to not pay any other bills with this money without a Court order, to deposit the remaining money into the Chase blocked account immediately, to file a receipt for deposit of the funds within one week and to account for what was spent (withdrawn) and show who the funds went to. The Court will not allow Ms. Carter to represent herself. Mr. Cowan is to remain as Counsel until there is a new Attorney who is willing to come in and deal with this case as well as Ms. Malveaux's case. The Court will also sign an order directing Wells Fargo Bank and Chase Bank to provide all statements to Mr. Cowan. Ms. Carter is to speak to Mr. Lamerdin regarding the new proposed Beneficiary. The matter was continued to February 2, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of the receipt on the blocked funds and the new proposal of the Beneficiary.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Return Of Funds By Regnaldo Woods And Bethel Ame Church
Off Calendar Jan-26-2011 Continued To Feb-02-11 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Amend Order To Distribute The Trust Property Of The John Douimbia And Evelyn Raymond 1996 Revocable Trust
Off Calendar Jan-19-2011 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christopher Lamerdin for Attorney General's Office. Regnaldo Woods in Propria Persona. Arlanders Etheley (Foster Care Provider) also present.

The Court stated that Mr. Cowan contacted the Court stating that he was out state and that he submitted the matter on his pleadings. The Court also stated that the difficulty was that Mr. Woods does not have standing. The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions. The Court denied the Petition to Amend Order to Distribute the Trust Assets. Mr. Lamerdin will prepare the order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

The California Attorney Generals Opposition To, And Motion To Strike, Petition To Amend Order To Distribution The Trust Property Of The John Douimbia And Evelyn Raymond 1996 Revocable Trust
Off Calendar Jan-19-2011 Text Ruling

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Return Of Funds By Regnaldo Woods And Bethel Ame Church
Off Calendar Dec-14-2010 Continued To Jan-26-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Cowan for Constance Carter. Christopher Lamerdin for Attorney General's Office.

Mr. Cowan stated that he obtained the certified checks from the Attorney General and Bethel Church. Mr. Cowan will contact the Bank to find out how to re-deposit the cashiers checks into the blocked account as he was the original purchaser of those checks, or if the payees need to endorse them. The matter was continued to January 26, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Successor Trustee C. Carter'S Petition For Attorney'S Fees.
Off Calendar Dec-14-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Cowan for and with Constance Carter. Chris Lamerdin from the Attorney General's Office. Edgar Boyd (Pastor) and Bobby Sisk from Bethel AME Church. Regnaldo Woods from Up From Darkness.

Mr. Cowan stated that the Parties met, that they did not resolve the matter, and that Ms. Carter wanted to start over and designate a new recipient of the funds that would meet the criteria. Ms. Carter expressed dissapointment/discouragement with the recipients actions. The Court ordered Mr. Woods and Bethel AME Church to return the funds within 10 days which are to be deposited into the blocked account. Status of the return of said funds was set for December 14, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition for Attorney's Fees was taken under submission for verification of the petition. If the petition is not verified by December 14, 2010, the matter will be continued to January 26, 2011. Presentation of the new recipient of the funds and filing of a declaration by Mr. Cowan regarding the set aside amount for Ms. Malveaux's claim was set for January 26, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Successor Trustee C. Carter'S Petition For Attorney'S Fees.
Off Calendar Nov-03-2010 Continued To Dec-01-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Cowan for and with Constance Carter. Christopher Lamerdin for the Attorney General's Office. Marianne Malveaux in Propria Persona. Reynaldo Woods from Up From Darkness in Propria Persona.

The Court returned a packet of papers to Ms. Carter stating that it could not accept them as all Parties had not received copies and that she should file them if she wanted the Court to consider them. Mr. Cowan stated that he distributed two checks, one to Reverend Woods and one to Pastor Boyd. Ms. Malveaux requested a continuance so that she could put her objection to the Attorney's fees with regard to the litigation matter in writing. Mr. Lamerdin stated that the Church needed to come up with a program to meet the spirit of the original trust and that they may need to step in if the Church could not do so. The Court stated that there was an issue with the petition with regard to the verification and the Proof of Service, and that it would not make a ruling today. The matter was continued to December 1, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a further report of status of the funds being used appropriately or whether the funds need to be returned. Mr. Cowan's request to be allowed to address the issue of verification of the petition in private will be addressed at the next hearing.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Successor Trustee C. Carter'S Petition For Attorney'S Fees.
Off Calendar Oct-20-2010 Continued To Nov-03-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Determination Of Entitlement To Estate Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-22-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christopher Lamerdin for State of California Attorney General's Office. Robert Cowan for and with Constance Carter. Reginald Woods from Bethel AME Church also present.

Mr. Lamerdin stated that all of the details had been worked out. The Court approved the petition, and the fees and ordered $35,750.00 be set aside for defense of the civil lawsuit involving Ms. Malveaux (subject to the written order)

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Petition For Determination Of Entitlement To Estate Distribution

Petition For Award Of Reasonable Attorney Fees; Declaration Of Marianne Malveaux In Support Of Petition For Attorneys Fee
Off Calendar Apr-29-2010 Text Ruling

Petition For Determination Of Entitlement To Estate Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-24-2010 Continued To May-18-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Cowan for and with Constance Carter. Marianne Malvaeaux (former Counsel for Constance Carter) in Propria Persona. Christopher Lamerdin from the Attorney General's Office. Reverand Woods also present.

Ms. Malvaeaux stated that she signed the Substitution of Attorney and gave to Counsel this morning. Mr. Cowan submitted an alternate proposed order, stated that the Attorney General may have questions about the accounting, and that funds should be set aside for the purpose of paying for defense as to how funds were previously distributed. Mr. Lamerdin stated that that he was agreeable to the proposed distribution and to the overall plan, that he would to track the funds from the time of sale of the house to today. Ms. Malvaeaux stated that she was unsucessful at obtaining the HUD settlement statement from the title company and explained how the statement broke down the sale amount, expenses for the medical payment and the reverse mortgage pay off. Mr. Cowan stated that he had not reviewed the documents/receipts and suggested a full accounting be done in the event it became necessary at a later date. The Court stated that there was no petition before the Court for an accounting, that Mr. Lamerdin could ask Ms. Malvaeaux for an accounting. If not, file a petition for an accounting or that Mr. Cowan could file an accounting. Ms. Malvaeaux stated that she responded to the Examiner's notes today. The Petition for Determination of Entitlement was continued to May 18, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to deal with the accounting issues and details of the distribution. The Petition for Attorney's Fees was taken under submission. The Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel was taken off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel.
Off Calendar Mar-24-2010 Off Calendar

Petition For Determination Of Entitlement To Estate Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-24-2010 Continued To Mar-24-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marianne Malveaux in Propria Persona and for and with Constance Carter. Christopher Lamerdin from the Attorney General's Office for the State of California. Ronald Woods also present.

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to distribution to the charity, fees and Ms. Malveaux being relieved as Counsel. The Court suggested Mr. Woods set up a particular fund for a particular purpose in writing and provide it to Mr. Lamerdin. Mr. Woods and Mr. Lamerdin will work together on the language of the charity. The Court informed Ms. Malveaux that it would like to see an itemization of hours at the hourly rate. All matters were continued to March 24, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The proposed orders are to be submitted to the Court no later than two days in advance of the hearing date.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
